cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about why Americans aren't taking all their vacation days. The question is asking for the reason, and the options are A to D. First, I need to read through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points. The main points are: 1. More than half of Americans don't use all their vacation days despite feeling they need more holidays. 2. Many who do take breaks still check emails frequently, even daily or hourly. 3. Samuel Nahmias mentions the economic situation with high unemployment (9.6%) leading to people being unwilling to take time off. 4. People are more cautious about taking time off because they don't want to look bad in the current job climate. 5. Technology allows them to stay connected, so they feel the need to check in even when on vacation. Now, looking at the options: A: They devote themselves to work (workaholics). The passage does mention "workaholics," so this is a possible answer. B: Afraid of losing jobs. The economic situation and high unemployment are mentioned, leading people to not want to take time off because they fear losing their jobs. This also seems plausible. C: Asked to keep in touch with bosses. The passage says that some check emails, but it's not that they're asked to do so. It's more about their own concern or the culture of staying connected. So maybe not C. D: Not in need of holidays. The first sentence says they think they need more, so D is contradicted by the passage. Now, between A and B. The passage says "from a general perspective, this has a lot to do with the economic situation in the US. More and more people are unemployed." It also says that people are unwilling to take time off because of the job market. The term "workaholics" is mentioned, but that might be a result of the fear of losing jobs or the economic pressure. The workers are taking less time
